company.name: Meridian Retail Co.
company.project_description: Deploy an AI Tier-1 support assistant to deflect routine tickets (order status, returns eligibility, password resets) before they reach human agents.

flagged_assumptions.text: assumption, not validated: Deflection: 50% overall with 60% tier1 share implies ~83.3% tier1 deflection: Company docs show Tier1 Deflection Rate = 0.5 (50%), not 83.3%. The math implies 83.3% tier1 deflection, which exceeds the cs_tier1_deflection benchmark of ~68%. The company's own measured rate (50%) is below the benchmark, not above it. The claim misrepresents the company's evidence. (ref: cs_tier1_deflection)

assumption, not validated: Inference/operating budget: $48,000 vs build $380,000: The ratio of $48K inference to $380K build is only 0.126x (12.6%), far below the htec_true_op_cost benchmark of 3-5x. This suggests either the inference budget is incomplete (missing integration, model updates, scale factors) or the build estimate is inflated. Neither aligns with the benchmark. (ref: htec_true_op_cost)
assumption, not validated: hosting_architecture=on_prem (user unknown; branch A_on_prem)

output sanity check: Computed payback (1.7 months) is below the minimum realistic threshold (6 months) established across all benchmark sources. This suggests the implementation cost estimate may be understated relative to the company's operational scale — recommend validating the cost estimate before relying on this projection.

citations.claim: Customer-service chatbots commonly resolve about 68% of Tier-1 tickets when scoped to routine intents.
citations.source: Customer service chatbot resolution benchmarks (published industry figures)
citations.text: [cs_tier1_deflection] Customer-service chatbots commonly resolve about 68% of Tier-1 tickets when scoped to routine intents. (source: Customer service chatbot resolution benchmarks (published industry figures))

citations.claim: True operating cost of an AI system is often 3–5x the initial build estimate once inference at scale, integration, and model-update cycles are included.
citations.text: [htec_true_op_cost] True operating cost of an AI system is often 3–5x the initial build estimate once inference at scale, integration, and model-update cycles are included. (source: HTEC AI cost / ROI framework)

explainability.raw:
Cost impact: Driven by annual ticket volume (480K), cost per ticket ($12), Tier 1 share (60%), claimed Tier 1 deflection (83.3%), build cost ($380K), inference budget ($48K), plus modeled integration ($171K–$231K/yr) and model-update ($46K–$68K/yr) costs. Flagged assumptions: Tier 1 deflection contradicts company's own 50% measured rate and exceeds 68% benchmark; inference-to-build ratio (0.13×) far below 3–5× benchmark, suggesting missing operating costs; payback of 1.7 months violates 6-month minimum threshold. Confidence: 35.

Quality impact: Driven solely by claimed Tier 1 deflection rate (83.3%) as a proxy for resolution quality; no separate quality metrics modeled. Flagged assumption: Same deflection-rate mismatch — company evidence shows 50% Tier 1 deflection, not 83.3%, and benchmark caps near 68%. Confidence: 30.

Speed to value: Driven by Year-1 cost ($623K) versus annual value ($2.0M–$3.3M) yielding payback of 1.5–2.6 months across scenarios. Flagged assumption: Payback falls well below the 6-month realistic minimum from all benchmarks, indicating likely understated implementation costs. Confidence: 25.

Process impact: Driven by ticket volume (480K) × Tier 1 share (60%) × claimed deflection rate (83.3%) producing 168K–274K deflected tickets annually. Flagged assumption: Deflection rate inflated versus company's 50% measured and 68% benchmark, overstating process throughput gains. Confidence: 40.

Technology impact: Driven by on-prem architecture assumption; build cost ($380K), inference budget ($48K), integration ($171K–$231K/yr), and model-update ($46K–$68K/yr) costs. Flagged assumptions: Inference budget ratio (0.13× build) far below 3–5× benchmark, suggesting incomplete operating-cost capture; hosting architecture itself is an unvalidated branch assumption. Confidence: 45.

Overall confidence: Low — multiple critical inputs (deflection rate, operating cost ratio, payback period) conflict with company evidence and external benchmarks, requiring validation before reliance.

flagged_assumptions.text: assumption, not validated: Deflection: 50% overall (tier1_share=60%, implied tier1=83.3%): Internal inconsistency: company data shows Tier1 Deflection Rate = 50%, but claim's math implies 83.3%. Also, 50% overall exceeds typical 20-35% range, and even the 'implied' 83.3% tier1 exceeds the 68% benchmark for routine intents. (ref: cs_tier1_deflection)

assumption, not validated: Inference/operating budget: $48,000 vs build $380,000: Company's $48K inference is only ~12.6% of $380K build—opposite of the benchmark, which states true operating cost is often 3-5x build. This suggests either severe underestimation of inference costs or that the $48K figure is incomplete. (ref: htec_true_op_cost)
assumption, not validated: hosting_architecture=cloud (user unknown; branch B_cloud)

output sanity check: Computed 3-year ROI (6.6x) exceeds the benchmark-realistic ceiling (5.8x) even after input-claim clamping. This suggests the implementation cost estimate may be understated relative to the company's operational scale — recommend validating the cost estimate before relying on this projection.

output sanity check: Computed payback (1.7 months) is below the minimum realistic threshold (6 months) established across all benchmark sources. This suggests the implementation cost estimate may be understated relative to the company's operational scale — recommend validating the cost estimate before relying on this projection.

explainability.raw:
**Cost impact**  
Driven by real company data: 480K annual tickets × $12/ticket, $380K build cost, and modeled integration/update costs. Flagged assumption: $48K annual inference budget is only 12.6% of build (benchmark: 3–5× build), suggesting severe operating cost underestimation. Confidence: 70.

**Quality impact**  
Driven by claimed 83.3% tier-1 deflection rate applied to 60% tier-1 ticket share. Flagged assumptions: internal inconsistency (company data shows 50% tier-1 deflection vs. 83.3% implied), and both exceed benchmarks (20–35% overall, 68% routine tier-1). Confidence: 40.

**Speed to value**  
Driven by payback of 1.7–2.5 months from modeled cost savings vs. year-1 cost of $585K. Flagged assumptions: inference cost likely understated (inflating speed), deflection rate likely overstated (inflating savings), and payback falls below 6-month minimum threshold. Confidence: 50.

**Process impact**  
Driven by real 480K volume and 60% tier-1 share; deflection reduces agent-handled tickets by 168K–274K annually. Flagged assumption: deflection magnitude relies on unvalidated 83.3% tier-1 rate, overstating process change. Confidence: 65.

**Technology impact**  
Driven by $380K build (real), cloud hosting (assumed), and $48K inference (flagged). Flagged assumptions: inference budget inconsistent with 3–5× build benchmark; cloud architecture unspecified by user. Confidence: 55.

Overall confidence: 56 — projections are directionally useful but materially sensitive to unvalidated deflection and operating cost assumptions.

recommendation.reasoning: Scenario B (Cloud) is recommended. In the likely case, B delivers 6.61x ROI vs A's 5.69x, and B's 3-year cost is $1.14M vs A's $1.29M—a $157K cost advantage. Both scenarios share the same two core assumption issues (deflection math inconsistency and inference budget ratio), but B has one additional flag: its ROI (6.6x) exceeds the benchmark-realistic ceiling (5.8x), suggesting cost estimates may be understated. However, this overage is modest and B still carries an overall confidence of 56%—a quantifiable signal absent for A (null). The financial gap is material enough to prefer B, but CFO should insist on validating the cost model before final approval given the benchmark ceiling breach.
recommendation.confidence_caveat: Recommendation carries moderate risk: Scenario B's overall confidence is 56%, and both branches share unvalidated core assumptions on deflection rates and inference budgets that could shift ROI materially if corrected.
recommendation.text: Scenario B (Cloud) is recommended. In the likely case, B delivers 6.61x ROI vs A's 5.69x, and B's 3-year cost is $1.14M vs A's $1.29M—a $157K cost advantage. Both scenarios share the same two core assumption issues (deflection math inconsistency and inference budget ratio), but B has one additional flag: its ROI (6.6x) exceeds the benchmark-realistic ceiling (5.8x), suggesting cost estimates may be understated. However, this overage is modest and B still carries an overall confidence of 56%—a quantifiable signal absent for A (null). The financial gap is material enough to prefer B, but CFO should insist on validating the cost model before final approval given the benchmark ceiling breach. Recommendation carries moderate risk: Scenario B's overall confidence is 56%, and both branches share unvalidated core assumptions on deflection rates and inference budgets that could shift ROI materially if corrected.
